pages.text: Late weapons are not unlocked by a short quest. They are tied to $(l)Branded Progress$(), which rises only while you are $(l)Branded$() and actively enduring the burden through real play. The default requirement for the true weapon gate is $(l)99.5%$() of the full progress track.

pages.title: Branded Time Checker
pages.text: Craft a $(l)Branded Time Checker$() with a $(item)Book$() and a $(item)Gold Ring$(). This tool helps track your Branded Progress and whether your burden is great enough to meet the late-game weapon gate.
The $(l)Branded Time Checker$() reveals your current Branded Progress, your ratio toward full attunement, and whether the burden is sufficient for the late-game weapons.
pages.title: Branded Time Checker

pages.text: $(l)Shared rule for Sloth, Pride, and Envy$()$(br)All three current sin weapons use the same Branded Progress gate. In creative mode, the config can optionally bypass that requirement for testing.

$(l)What counts$()$(br)Branded Progress is meant to reflect $(l)active play$(), not idle waiting. Moving, fighting, mining, and otherwise surviving beneath the Oath will advance it. Standing still and going AFK will not meaningfully satisfy the burden.

$(l)Practical advice$()$(br)If players ask why a weapon is locked, the short answer is almost always: $(br)$(li)You must be Branded$(li)You must have endured enough active Branded Progress$(li)The weapon will stay dormant until both are true
